where did the idea of a messiah come from The concept of a messiah in Western religious thought primarily originated within Christianity, which developed this belief as part of its theological framework. This idea was further influenced by earlier Jewish eschatological expectations and prophetic traditions, but it found its definitive form and widespread acceptance through Christian doctrine.

where did the idea of a messiah come from The concept of a messiah in Christian theology originated within Christianity, where Jesus Christ is recognized as the prophesied Messiah or Savior sent by God to save humanity.
